fileNodes.name: fb_ads_library.py
fileNodes.summary: Wraps the Facebook Ads Library API to search competitor ads (by keyword or page id), resolve Facebook page URLs to page ids, and format ad results for downstream LLM analysis.

fileNodes.name: fb_marketing.py
fileNodes.summary: Wraps the Facebook Marketing API to fetch account/ad-level insights, manage campaigns and ad sets (status changes, budget updates), and format performance data for LLM-based analysis and the ads optimizer.

fileNodes.name: image_gen.py
fileNodes.summary: Wraps an AI image generation/editing provider to generate marketing images, edit existing images, analyze image style, and estimate generation cost.

fileNodes.name: llm_router.py
fileNodes.summary: Multi-provider LLM router abstracting Anthropic, Gemini, OpenAI, and Perplexity behind a unified call() interface with task-type-based provider selection, fallback chains, and availability reporting.

fileNodes.name: video_vision.py
fileNodes.summary: Extracts keyframes from uploaded videos via ffmpeg probing/extraction and analyzes them with an AI vision model to produce a visual analysis summary for marketing content review.

fileNodes.name: feedback_digest.py
fileNodes.summary: Background worker that formats and runs a periodic digest job summarizing recent user feedback for review.

fileNodes.name: monitor_competitors.py
fileNodes.summary: Background worker that periodically checks tracked competitors' Facebook ad activity via the Ads Library and notifies the bot/user of new ads detected.

fileNodes.name: brand_voice.py
fileNodes.summary: Storage layer for brand voice profiles: reads/writes versioned brand voice records (tone, rules, banned words) to the database and supports deactivation.

fileNodes.name: campaign_history.py
fileNodes.summary: Storage layer for past campaign runs: builds and saves campaign summaries with embeddings, and supports semantic similarity search plus listing past campaigns.

fileNodes.name: embeddings.py
fileNodes.summary: Thin wrapper around the OpenAI embeddings API to convert text into vector embeddings for semantic search.

fileNodes.name: feedback_log.py
fileNodes.summary: Storage layer for user feedback: logs feedback events, retrieves recent feedback, and builds digest summaries for periodic reporting.

fileNodes.name: session.py
fileNodes.summary: Core session persistence layer: manages the DB connection pool, normalizes/migrates v1-to-v2 session row formats, and provides get/save/reset operations for user sessions.

fileNodes.name: tracked_competitors.py
fileNodes.summary: Storage layer for competitor tracking: add/remove tracked competitors, list by user, and find competitors due for a periodic ad-monitoring check.

fileNodes.name: crypto.py
fileNodes.summary: Provides Fernet-based symmetric encryption/decryption helpers for securely storing sensitive tokens (e.g. Facebook OAuth tokens) at rest.

fileNodes.name: max_conductor.py
fileNodes.summary: Routes free-form user messages to an intent + parameters using an Anthropic LLM call, used to dispatch follow-up requests in the Telegram bot (e.g. 'change channel to Instagram').

fileNodes.name: post_actions.py
fileNodes.summary: Parses generated content calendars into structured posts and provides LLM-backed actions (edit, adapt to channel, generate variant) plus preview formatting for Telegram.

fileNodes.name: tone_calibration.py
fileNodes.summary: Implements the brand-voice tone calibration flow: parses a sample post from a calendar, generates sample posts, extracts tone signals from user feedback, and regenerates content/calendars to match the calibrated tone.

fileNodes.name: config.py
fileNodes.summary: Central configuration module loading environment variables and constants (API keys, tokens, feature flags) used across the entire application.

fileNodes.name: run_local.py
fileNodes.summary: Local entrypoint for the Telegram bot: initializes storage pools/DB, registers all command/message/callback handlers on a python-telegram-bot Application, and runs polling.

fileNodes.name: fb_oauth.py
fileNodes.summary: Implements the Facebook OAuth flow: builds the authorization URL, exchanges/extends access tokens, fetches ad accounts, handles the OAuth callback, and notifies the user in Telegram once connected.

fileNodes.name: __init__.py
fileNodes.summary: Package init for the storage module; re-exports brand voice, campaign history, embeddings, models, and session storage submodules for convenient imports.

fileNodes.name: __init__.py
fileNodes.summary: Package marker for the agents module that re-exports the pipeline orchestrator as the public entry point.

fileNodes.name: agent_wrappers.py
fileNodes.summary: Thin async wrapper functions around the strategic AI skills (market research, competitor analysis, pricing, SWOT, retention/winback, synthesis) that adapt provider calls into the pipeline's agent interface.

fileNodes.name: content_suite_prompts.py
fileNodes.summary: Static prompt template constants for the content generation suite (posts, captions, video scripts) consumed by operational skills.

fileNodes.name: critic.py
fileNodes.summary: Runs an LLM-based quality critic pass over agent output, detecting meta-review artifacts and injecting hyperlinks to known sources before returning a reviewed result.

fileNodes.name: operational_prompts.py
fileNodes.summary: Large library of static prompt template constants for operational marketing skills (ads, sales scripts, email/Zalo sequences, UGC briefs).

fileNodes.name: operational_skill.py
fileNodes.summary: Defines the base OperationalSkillConfig and OperationalSkill classes that establish the contract (context building, user message building, intake field tracking) for all operational marketing skills.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Uses a dataclass-style config object paired with a base class implementing template-method style hooks overridden by concrete skill subclasses defined elsewhere.

fileNodes.name: operational_skills_config.py
fileNodes.summary: Central registry and factory module defining all concrete operational skill classes (campaign briefs, content calendars, ads copy, video scripts, competitor comparison, brand positioning) and a lookup function to instantiate them by task name.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Large module aggregating ~10 skill subclasses and ~24 factory/helper functions; a strong candidate for splitting by skill domain in future refactors.

fileNodes.name: output_formats.py
fileNodes.summary: Defines output-format and language-instruction string templates used to steer LLM response structure (e.g. bullet lists, tables) and localization level.

fileNodes.name: pipeline.py
fileNodes.summary: Core orchestration module that sequences the multi-agent marketing strategy pipeline — intake, market research, competitor analysis, pricing, SWOT, retention/winback, synthesis, and operational skill execution — and persists results to the session/history store.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Acts as the central coordinator that wires together skills, critics, and storage; one of the largest and most structurally significant files in the project.

fileNodes.name: prompts.py
fileNodes.summary: Large library of static system/user prompt template constants for the strategic pipeline stages (intake, market research, competitor, customer, pricing, social).

fileNodes.name: skills.py
fileNodes.summary: Defines the AgentSkill base class and concrete strategic skill classes (market research, competitor, customer insight, pricing psychology, social listening, USP, SWOT, strategy synthesis, tactical playbook) that build LLM context and prompts for each strategic stage.

fileNodes.name: strategy_prompts.py
fileNodes.summary: Builds the consolidated strategy-synthesis user prompt by combining prior stage outputs (brief, briefs-to-block formatting) into a single LLM input.

fileNodes.name: handlers.py
fileNodes.summary: The Telegram bot's primary command and message/callback dispatch module — implements all slash commands, free-text conversation handling, photo/document upload handling, and the large multi-step UI flows for intake, strategy consultation, content/campaign generation, brand voice calibration, and ads management.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Extremely large module (~10k lines, 141 functions); only the public command/dispatch functions are modeled as nodes here — the bulk of private step-handlers are omitted from the graph for conciseness but are summarized at the file level.

fileNodes.name: keyboards.py
fileNodes.summary: Builds Telegram inline keyboard layouts (action menus, stage-completion controls, post-action buttons) used throughout the bot's conversational UI.

fileNodes.name: renderers.py
fileNodes.summary: Converts structured/strategic and operational LLM outputs into user-facing presentation formats — Telegram message cards, Markdown report files, and multi-sheet Excel templates with table parsing and reformatting helpers.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Mixes Markdown table parsing with openpyxl-style Excel generation, including LLM-assisted table repair (_haiku_rebuild_table) for malformed model output.

fileNodes.name: __init__.py
fileNodes.summary: Package marker for the frameworks module that re-exports the KPI library, SAVE framework, and SMART framework helpers as the public entry point.

fileNodes.name: industry_context.py
fileNodes.summary: Defines a static library of industry-specific marketing context (search seeds, purchase archetypes, key-message hints) and helper functions to look up and format that context for prompts.

fileNodes.name: kpi_library.py
fileNodes.summary: Defines the KPIFramework data model and a static library of industry-specific KPI sets (primary/secondary/vanity metrics, benchmarks, unit economics) with lookup and text-formatting helpers.

fileNodes.name: save_framework.py
fileNodes.summary: Implements the SAVE marketing framework analysis generator, producing a structured solution/access/value/education breakdown for a given business and industry.

fileNodes.name: smart_framework.py
fileNodes.summary: Implements SMART goal-setting templates and formatting helpers, scaling goal targets by business stage for inclusion in strategy prompts.

fileNodes.name: campaign_intelligence.py
fileNodes.summary: Logs campaign intelligence events (brief edits, stage transitions) to Supabase for downstream analytics on user campaign behavior.

fileNodes.name: __init__.py
fileNodes.summary: Package barrel for the storage v2 layer, re-exporting CRUD functions from briefs, campaigns, engagements, posts, profiles, sessions, skill runs, strategies, and users modules.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel (__init__.py) aggregating the public API of the storage/v2 subpackage.

fileNodes.name: _client.py
fileNodes.summary: Provides a shared factory for obtaining the Supabase client instance used across all storage/v2 data access modules.

fileNodes.name: briefs.py
fileNodes.summary: Data access functions for creating and retrieving strategy briefs, including fetching the latest brief for an engagement.

fileNodes.name: campaigns_v2.py
fileNodes.summary: CRUD operations for marketing campaigns, including creation, retrieval, listing by user/status, and updating campaign embeddings.

fileNodes.name: engagements.py
fileNodes.summary: Manages engagement records representing a user's marketing consulting session, including creation, lookup of active/latest engagements, updates, and listing.

fileNodes.name: posts.py
fileNodes.summary: Data access layer for content calendar posts, supporting insertion, retrieval, updates, listing by campaign or user, and soft deletion.

fileNodes.name: profiles.py
fileNodes.summary: CRUD functions for user marketing profiles, supporting retrieval, deletion, and partial-field upserts.

fileNodes.name: sessions_slim.py
fileNodes.summary: Manages lightweight conversation session state for the Telegram bot, including default session creation, upsert, deletion, and last-message timestamp tracking.

fileNodes.name: skill_runs.py
fileNodes.summary: Records and retrieves AI skill execution runs (versioned outputs, ratings, and feedback) used by the marketing agent pipeline.

fileNodes.name: strategies.py
fileNodes.summary: Persists and retrieves marketing strategy documents (positioning, roadmap, budget, KPIs) tied to an engagement, with versioning and rating support.

fileNodes.name: users.py
fileNodes.summary: User account data access layer handling profile upserts, soft deletion, industry cache clearing, and token quota/usage tracking.

fileNodes.label: campaign_execution.py
fileNodes.summary: Generates a tactical execution plan for a campaign by classifying its goal type, mapping it to a funnel objective, and calling the LLM router to produce a structured plan, with a deterministic fallback plan when generation fails.

fileNodes.label: campaign_ideation.py
fileNodes.summary: Largest agent module in the batch; drives the campaign ideation pipeline end to end -- generating discovery questions, synthesizing campaign options from research, proposing offer packages and levers, and parsing/formatting the dynamic finalize form into brief fields.

fileNodes.label: campaign_intake.py
fileNodes.summary: Drives the campaign intake conversation: parses completion signals, builds a campaign draft from strategy data, generates brand voice, and renders draft/confirmation cards.

fileNodes.label: discovery.py
fileNodes.summary: Runs the business discovery conversation and research pipeline: parses completion, fills research seeds, runs per-topic deep-dive research, assembles results, and generates/persists a diagnostic brief.

fileNodes.label: funnel_mapper.py
fileNodes.summary: Generates and renders a marketing funnel map (stages, content, channels) from strategy/campaign context, including Markdown and Excel (openpyxl) export formats.

fileNodes.label: orchestrator.py
fileNodes.summary: Coordinates the multi-agent strategic pipeline: runs tiers of agents in sequence/parallel with abort handling, defines pipeline configuration data classes, and includes a self-test smoke-test harness with mock agents.

fileNodes.label: strategy.py
fileNodes.summary: Generates the overall business marketing strategy via LLM router call grounded in industry brief and business profile, persists/retrieves strategy versions, renders a strategy card, and runs the top-level advisor flow combining research, brief, and strategy generation.

fileNodes.label: workflow_runner.py
fileNodes.summary: Runs the 'write content' operational workflow: resolves or generates a brand direction (with deterministic fallback) and drives the content-writing skill execution with progress callbacks.

fileNodes.label: models.py
fileNodes.summary: Central data-model module defining the core domain objects -- BusinessProfile, BrandVoice, VersionedResult, and Session -- plus TaskType/PipelineStage enumerations. Widely imported across the codebase as the foundational session/profile state representation.

fileNodes.label: session_v2_adapter.py
fileNodes.summary: Adapter layer bridging the in-memory Session/BusinessProfile model to the v2 persistence backend -- loads a full session (user, profile, slim session state, skill runs) and saves session state back to storage.

fileNodes.label: test_orchestrator_e2e.py
fileNodes.summary: End-to-end test suite for the multi-agent orchestrator pipeline, using a MockBehavior helper to stub skill/router calls and exercising happy-path execution, error handling, and a latency benchmark.

fileNodes.name: api.py
fileNodes.summary: Defines the full Starlette route table for the web dashboard REST/SSE API, covering state bootstrap, live streaming updates, business-data endpoints (skill runs, content, intake, agent runs, ads), chat, and CRUD operations for tracked items, campaigns, calendar posts, reports, accounts, and usage quotas.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Large single-file route module aggregating dozens of thin async Starlette endpoint handlers, most delegating to the store/biz/tg modules and wrapping results in JSONResponse.

fileNodes.name: main.py
fileNodes.summary: Webhook-mode entry point for the Telegram bot; builds the python-telegram-bot Application, registers all command/message/callback handlers, mounts the web dashboard and OAuth callback routes on a single Starlette+uvicorn server, and manages startup/shutdown lifecycle (DB pool, webhook registration, background monitors).
fileNodes.languageNotes: Uses Starlette's asynccontextmanager-based lifespan to coordinate Telegram Application startup/shutdown alongside background asyncio tasks (monitor, ads scheduler).

fileNodes.name: web_control.py
fileNodes.summary: Telegram command handlers that let users inspect and control the web dashboard state (status, campaigns, tracked items, optimizations, alerts) directly from chat.

fileNodes.name: run_web.py
fileNodes.summary: Standalone entry point for running just the web dashboard server (no Telegram bot token required), defaulting to a SQLite-backed store with optional Supabase backend and realtime listener.

fileNodes.name: test_web_api.py
fileNodes.summary: Unit tests verifying that the web dashboard API exposes its core and editor routes and that business-data endpoints degrade gracefully when Supabase is unavailable.

fileNodes.name: __init__.py
fileNodes.summary: Empty package marker file for the webapp package.

fileNodes.name: events.py
fileNodes.summary: Implements the pub/sub event Hub used for Server-Sent Events streaming, plus background tasks that poll a state snapshot function and listen to Supabase realtime changes to push live dashboard updates.

fileNodes.name: store.py
fileNodes.summary: Facade module that selects between a SQLite or Supabase storage backend at runtime based on environment configuration, and forwards all dashboard data operations (tracked items, jobs, campaigns, calendar, reports, accounts, quotas) to the active backend.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Backend-switching facade pattern: all public functions are thin async pass-throughs to whichever module (_sqlite or supabase) is selected via configure().

fileNodes.name: store_supabase.py
fileNodes.summary: Supabase REST-backed implementation of the dashboard storage interface (mirrors store_sqlite), handling client creation, demo-data seeding, and CRUD operations for tracked items, jobs, campaigns, calendar posts, reports, accounts, and usage quotas in `web_`-prefixed Supabase tables.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Mirrors the sqlite backend's async interface exactly so webapp/store.py can swap backends transparently; uses supabase-py's AsyncClient with REST table() calls.

fileNodes.name: manager_personas.py
fileNodes.summary: Defines the ManagerPersona data model and routing logic that selects which marketing 'manager' persona (and orchestration context) should handle a user's message, plus rendering helpers for persona intro/team cards.

fileNodes.name: task_registry.py
fileNodes.summary: Defines the TaskConfig model and a static registry of marketing tasks (e.g. content calendar, brand voice), including intake-field requirements and lookup helpers used to drive the bot's task selection flow.

fileNodes.name: __init__.py
fileNodes.summary: Empty package initializer marking the bot/ directory as a Python package.

fileNodes.name: test_persona_menu_changes.py
fileNodes.summary: Test suite covering persona menu/keyboard behavior, task registry lookups, xlsx import/export round-tripping, key-message hint generation, video script formatting, and brand-voice do/don't rule extraction.

fileNodes.name: deploy-pages.yml
fileNodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that publishes the static web/ demo dashboard for the Marketing OS project to GitHub Pages on pushes to main or the web-app-interface branch, or via manual dispatch.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Single-job workflow using path filters (web/**) and a concurrency group to ensure only one Pages deploy runs at a time.

fileNodes.name: deploy
fileNodes.summary: Job that checks out the repo, configures GitHub Pages, uploads the web/ directory as a Pages artifact, and deploys it via actions/deploy-pages.

fileNodes.name: sessions
fileNodes.summary: Initial migration creating the sessions table that tracks each user's onboarding stage, intake history, profile, and accumulated results, with supporting indexes on stage and update time.

fileNodes.name: tracked_competitors
fileNodes.summary: Migration creating the tracked_competitors table used to monitor competitor ad activity per user, storing the last seen ad IDs and indexed for due-for-check polling.

fileNodes.name: feedback_log
fileNodes.summary: Migration creating the feedback_log table that records user ratings and feedback text on generated skill outputs, indexed by skill/industry and by low ratings for quality review.

fileNodes.name: user_brand_voice
fileNodes.summary: Migration creating the user_brand_voice table that versions and stores each user's brand voice profile, with an active-version flag and an index for quick lookup by user.

fileNodes.name: user_campaign_history
fileNodes.summary: Migration enabling the pgvector extension and creating the user_campaign_history table, which stores past campaign briefs and an embedding column for similarity search across industry, stage, and competitor context.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Uses the pgvector extension to add a vector embedding column with a dedicated similarity index, enabling semantic retrieval of past campaign history.

fileNodes.name: users
fileNodes.summary: Large normalization migration that restructures the schema into six core tables (users, user_business_profile, user_sessions_slim, skill_runs, campaigns, posts) replacing earlier denormalized session data, with extensive indexing for active users, quotas, and skill/campaign lookups.
fileNodes.languageNotes: A large-scale normalization migration splitting a monolithic sessions table into six purpose-specific tables with foreign-key-style relations and 14 supporting indexes.

fileNodes.name: user_business_profile
fileNodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; stores each user's business profile attributes (industry, stage, target customer, revenue, budget, channels, competitors) split out from the original sessions table.

fileNodes.name: user_sessions_slim
fileNodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; a lean per-user session table tracking the currently selected task, intake history, and tone calibration, replacing the bulkier original sessions table.

fileNodes.name: skill_runs
fileNodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; logs each invocation of a marketing skill per user, including generated content, model used, and rating, indexed for recent and per-skill lookups.

fileNodes.name: campaigns
fileNodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; represents a marketing campaign per user with an offer lever, end date, embedding, and link back to the originating skill run, indexed for active-campaign queries.

fileNodes.name: posts
fileNodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; tracks individual scheduled content posts belonging to a campaign, with day, status, and link fields, indexed by user/channel and parent campaign.

fileNodes.name: engagements
fileNodes.summary: Migration introducing the engagement-centric data spine: creates engagements, diagnostic_briefs, and strategies tables to model a structured consulting workflow per user, replacing ad-hoc campaign tracking.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Introduces a hierarchical engagement -> diagnostic brief -> strategy data model with versioning and token-usage tracking, supported by 10 indexes.

fileNodes.name: diagnostic_briefs
fileNodes.summary: Part of the engagement spine migration; stores diagnostic brief documents generated for an engagement, tracking token usage and creation time.

fileNodes.name: strategies
fileNodes.summary: Part of the engagement spine migration; stores versioned marketing strategy documents tied to an engagement and diagnostic brief, including content pillars, KPI dashboard, model used, and user feedback rating.

fileNodes.name: user_sessions_slim (v2 fields)
fileNodes.summary: Small migration adding missing V2 columns (pending_followup_skill, content_outputs, token_log) to user_sessions_slim and a cost_used_usd column to users, fixing gaps from the V1-to-V2 session adapter cutover.

fileNodes.name: campaign_intelligence
fileNodes.summary: Migration creating the campaign_intelligence table that captures industry-specific campaign intelligence events per user, indexed by industry, event, and creation time.

fileNodes.name: user_fb_connections
fileNodes.summary: Migration creating Facebook integration tables: user_fb_connections for OAuth-linked ad accounts, oauth_states for the OAuth handshake, ads_snapshots for daily ad performance metrics, and ads_alert_cooldowns to throttle repeat alerts.

fileNodes.name: oauth_states
fileNodes.summary: Part of the Facebook connections migration; stores short-lived OAuth state tokens used to validate the Facebook OAuth callback flow per user.

fileNodes.name: ads_snapshots
fileNodes.summary: Part of the Facebook connections migration; stores daily snapshots of Facebook ad campaign performance metrics (impressions, reach, clicks, CTR, CPM, leads, purchases) per user, indexed by user and date.

fileNodes.name: ads_alert_cooldowns
fileNodes.summary: Part of the Facebook connections migration; tracks cooldown state per user/campaign/alert-type to prevent duplicate ad performance alerts from being sent repeatedly.

fileNodes.name: user_fb_connections (available_accounts)
fileNodes.summary: Tiny migration adding an available_accounts JSONB column to user_fb_connections so users can switch between Facebook ad accounts without re-authenticating via OAuth.

fileNodes.name: code-reviewer.md
fileNodes.summary: Claude Code subagent definition for a senior code-reviewer persona that evaluates changes across correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance, producing a categorized review output.

fileNodes.name: security-auditor.md
fileNodes.summary: Claude Code subagent definition for a security-auditor persona that reviews input handling, auth, data protection, infrastructure, and AI/LLM features, classifying findings by severity.

fileNodes.name: test-engineer.md
fileNodes.summary: Claude Code subagent definition for a QA/test-engineer persona that designs test suites, writes tests, and analyzes coverage gaps using a structured analyze-then-write approach.

fileNodes.name: web-performance-auditor.md
fileNodes.summary: Claude Code subagent definition for a web-performance-auditor persona covering Core Web Vitals, loading, rendering, and network optimization, with quick and deep (tool-assisted) operating modes.

fileNodes.name: build.md
fileNodes.summary: Defines the `/build` slash command, which implements plan tasks incrementally via a RED-GREEN-verify-commit loop, with an `auto` mode that runs the entire approved plan without stopping between tasks.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Claude Code slash-command markdown with YAML frontmatter description and `$ARGUMENTS` mode dispatch.

fileNodes.name: code-simplify.md
fileNodes.summary: Defines the `/code-simplify` slash command, which guides simplification of recently changed code while preserving behavior, covering nesting, function length, naming, duplication, and dead code.

fileNodes.name: plan.md
fileNodes.summary: Defines the `/plan` slash command, which breaks work into small verifiable tasks with acceptance criteria and dependency ordering, saving output to tasks/plan.md and tasks/todo.md.

fileNodes.name: review.md
fileNodes.summary: Defines the `/review` slash command, which conducts a five-axis code review covering correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance with categorized findings.

fileNodes.name: ship.md
fileNodes.summary: Defines the `/ship` slash command, a fan-out orchestrator that runs code-reviewer, security-auditor, and test-engineer subagents in parallel, then merges their reports into a single go/no-go launch decision with a mandatory rollback plan.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Documents a parallel multi-agent fan-out/merge pattern using Claude Code's Agent tool and subagent_type dispatch.

fileNodes.name: spec.md
fileNodes.summary: Defines the `/spec` slash command, which starts spec-driven development by gathering requirements and generating a structured SPEC.md before any code is written.

fileNodes.name: test.md
fileNodes.summary: Defines the `/test` slash command, which runs the test-driven development workflow for new features and the Prove-It pattern for bug fixes, including a hook to browser-based verification for UI issues.

fileNodes.name: webperf.md
fileNodes.summary: Defines the `/webperf` slash command, which runs a web performance audit via the web-performance-auditor subagent, supporting a Deep mode using Lighthouse/PSI/CrUX/DevTools data and a Quick mode based on static source scanning.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Defines the 'idea-refine' Claude Code skill: a three-phase (Understand & Expand, Evaluate & Converge, Sharpen & Ship) ideation partner that turns a vague idea into a concrete markdown one-pager with problem statement, recommended direction, assumptions, MVP scope, and a 'not doing' list.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Claude Code skill markdown with YAML frontmatter (name/description) followed by structured trigger phrases, process phases, anti-patterns, and a verification checklist.

fileNodes.name: examples.md
fileNodes.summary: Worked examples illustrating the idea-refine skill's three-phase process across a vague early-stage concept, a feature idea within an existing codebase, and a non-product workflow idea, ending with takeaways on what makes a good ideation session.

fileNodes.name: frameworks.md
fileNodes.summary: Reference catalog of ideation frameworks (SCAMPER, How Might We, First Principles, Jobs to Be Done, Constraint-Based Ideation, Pre-mortem, Analogous Inspiration) used to generate idea variations during Phase 1 of the idea-refine skill.

fileNodes.name: refinement-criteria.md
fileNodes.summary: Evaluation rubric for stress-testing idea directions during Phase 2 of the idea-refine skill, covering user value, feasibility, and differentiation dimensions, an assumption audit (must/should/might be true), and MVP scoping principles.

fileNodes.name: .env.example
fileNodes.summary: Template for required and optional runtime environment variables, covering Telegram bot credentials, AI provider keys (Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini), Supabase storage, Facebook Ads OAuth, admin access, and Railway deployment settings.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Vietnamese inline comments group variables by concern (required, AI providers, Facebook Ads, admin, deploy) and document where to obtain each credential.

fileNodes.name: DEPLOY_RAILWAY.md
fileNodes.summary: Vietnamese-language deployment guide for running the web dashboard (not the Telegram bot) on Railway, covering project setup, persistent storage options (SQLite vs Supabase vs Railway Volume), and optional Telegram notifications for web actions.

fileNodes.name: Procfile
fileNodes.summary: Process file declaring the web process that launches the Telegram bot entry point with PYTHONPATH set to the app root.

fileNodes.name: README.md
fileNodes.summary: Project overview for the Marketing OS Telegram bot describing its AI-powered marketing strategy features, installation steps, directory structure, and available bot commands.

fileNodes.name: index.html
fileNodes.summary: Large static HTML page, likely the web dashboard's front-end markup, bundling most of the UI structure and inline scripts/styles for the marketing OS web interface.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Nearly 2,900 lines in a single HTML file indicates a monolithic dashboard page rather than a componentized front-end build.

fileNodes.name: railway.json
fileNodes.summary: Railway platform configuration that overrides the default Procfile process, instructing Railway to build with Nixpacks and start the web dashboard via run_web.py with automatic restarts on failure.

fileNodes.name: requirements.txt
fileNodes.summary: Python dependency manifest listing core packages for the bot and web stack: Anthropic and OpenAI/Gemini SDKs, python-telegram-bot, Supabase client, ASGI server (uvicorn/starlette), cryptography, and supporting utilities.

fileNodes.name: ADS_SCHEDULER_SETUP.md
fileNodes.summary: Setup guide for the Ads Scheduler background job, covering database migration, encryption key generation, Facebook App OAuth configuration, Railway environment variables, deployment verification, and the available alert-threshold commands and benchmarks.

fileNodes.name: APP_FUNCTIONS.md
fileNodes.summary: Comprehensive functional reference for the Marketing OS bot describing overall architecture, Telegram commands, the Intake/Strategic/Operational/Analysis skill layers, pipelines and orchestrators, the Ads Scheduler reporting job, core background systems (LLM router, Industry Brain, Brand Voice, tone calibration, campaign history), and a migration checklist of what to carry into a new repo.

fileNodes.name: BACKLOG.md
fileNodes.summary: Running backlog log of completed product/engineering fixes and improvements (dated entries) covering competitor comparison, dead-code removal, ads generator gaps, content calendar restructuring, strategic-question flow dependencies, and budget/team-context sequencing changes.

fileNodes.name: DB_V2_MIGRATION_PLAN.md
fileNodes.summary: Step-by-step runbook for migrating to a v2 database schema via dual-write, covering Supabase migration application, backfill scripts, Railway branch switching, drift detection, cutover to v2 reads, post-cutover cleanup, and an emergency rollback matrix with success KPIs.

fileNodes.name: IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
fileNodes.summary: Workflow 2.0 implementation plan with locked architecture decisions (Strategic, Campaign Bridge, Brand Voice, Content Execution layers), a sprint-by-sprint breakdown (DB schema, USP layer, retention integration, campaign brief, brand voice persistence, tone calibration, multi-agent orchestrator), dependency/risk registers, and a final roadmap.

fileNodes.name: LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md
fileNodes.summary: Short pre-launch checklist enumerating security gate items that must be resolved before opening the product to paying customers.

fileNodes.name: MAX_CMO_REFERENCE.md
fileNodes.summary: Reference map of the 'Max' CMO agent persona's functions and skills, detailing the strategic A-to-Z pipeline sequence, the Strategy-to-Campaign bridge (discovery, ideation, campaign brief), post-brief funnel mapping and execution planning, Layer 3 team coordination, and the main bot handler entry points tied to Max.

fileNodes.name: SCALE_BACKLOG.md
fileNodes.summary: Backlog of future-facing items to tackle when scaling the product or preparing it for sale to C-level/agency customers, including economics/feasibility gating, closing the measurement loop, and a multi-skin Strategy Object (Owner/C-level/Agency) concept.

fileNodes.name: markos-flow.md
fileNodes.summary: End-to-end walkthrough of the MarkOS user journey across all layers: entry/onboarding, profile-check gate, multi-turn intake, the six-stage strategic pipeline, operational skills (content calendar, content generator pipeline), tone calibration, LLM routing, Excel rendering paths, rating/feedback loop, and the full state-machine flag inventory, plus a list of known gaps from a prior audit.

fileNodes.name: ARCHITECTURE.md
fileNodes.summary: Current-state architecture map for the markOS Web App: SPA frontend talking to a Starlette backend (webapp/api.py, store.py, business.py, chat.py, events.py, notify.py) that reuses the Telegram bot's storage/agents/tools layers against a shared Supabase project, plus dev (SQLite/mock) vs prod (Supabase/live) run modes, the API contract, SSE realtime design, and known technical debt (no auth, mock charts, chat persistence gaps).
fileNodes.languageNotes: Written in Vietnamese; documents a dual-mode (dev/prod) architecture with an ASCII system diagram and explicit data-boundary rules between dashboard-owned web_* tables and the bot's production tables.

fileNodes.name: DECISIONS.md
fileNodes.summary: ADR-lite decision log for the Web App with 29 numbered decisions (D-001 to D-029) covering architecture (pluggable SQLite/Supabase store, shared Supabase with the bot), product strategy (Max-centric vs form-first entry, packaging tiers, golden-path milestones), and process rules (no fake UI, auth deferred, content calendar model), each tagged with status.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Vietnamese ADR-lite format (one-line decisions in a table); later entries supersede or reverse earlier ones (e.g., D-026 reverses D-007), reflecting an append-only, never-delete decision history.

fileNodes.name: PRODUCT.md
fileNodes.summary: Product requirements draft (PRD) for the Web App defining the value proposition (founders chat with virtual CMO 'Max' to get a marketing strategy), target users segmented by subscription tier (Starter/Pro/Agency), core jobs-to-be-done, the v1 golden-path outcomes (diagnosis-to-strategy, then content production), experience principles, the monthly-subscription business model, and explicit out-of-scope items like auth and Ads optimization.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Vietnamese PRD with placeholder questions (❓) awaiting founder input; every feature is meant to trace back to a section in this file.

fileNodes.name: ROADMAP.md
fileNodes.summary: Milestone roadmap (M0-M4) for the Web App following a 'deepen one golden path before expanding' principle: M0 establishes one fully real end-to-end user flow, M1 completes the diagnosis-to-strategy stage, M2 ships real content production, M3 adds live Ads/competitor operations, and M4 delivers auth, billing, and observability for public readiness.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Vietnamese roadmap using checkbox task lists per milestone and an explicit priority rule set (fix golden path before adding pages).

fileNodes.name: WAY_OF_WORKING.md
fileNodes.summary: Working agreement between the Founder and the Engineer/CTO (Claude) defining five core principles (spec-first, vertical-slice depth, no fake UI, docs/web/ as single source of truth, decisions get recorded), the per-slice workflow loop, the Definition of Done checklist, quality/risk gates (LLM cost, security, privacy, FB API degradation), a 1-pager template, technical conventions, and triggers requiring the CTO to stop and ask the Founder.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Vietnamese process contract; codifies a spec-then-code, single-vertical-slice methodology with a reusable 1-pager template and explicit escalation triggers.

fileNodes.name: M0-directional-reframe.md
fileNodes.summary: Spec (D-029) reframing the M0 web synthesis output as strategic 'direction' rather than committed SMART numbers, covering intake field alignment, output presentation changes, and removal of dead Max-chat UI, with acceptance criteria and a Vietnamese-language plan to fix three identified inconsistencies between the web wizard and the bot's discovery/synthesis logic.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Written in Vietnamese; references specific code symbols (web/app.js INTAKE_STEPS, agents/discovery.REQUIRED_FIELDS) as evidence for the spec's acceptance criteria.

fileNodes.name: M0-discovery-to-strategy.md
fileNodes.summary: One-pager planning slice for milestone M0 describing the end-to-end golden path from chat-based business discovery through diagnostic pipeline execution to rendering a real 90-day strategy page, including scope, risks, dependencies, and founder-approved decisions.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Written in Vietnamese; lists explicit module dependencies (agents/discovery.py, agents/pipeline.py, agents/strategy.py, storage/v2/strategies.py, webapp/business.py, webapp/chat.py) as the slice's 'Phụ thuộc' section.

fileNodes.name: M1-content-production.md
fileNodes.summary: Draft one-pager for milestone M1 specifying the two-track (always-on vs. occasion-based campaign) content production model for the web app, including data model changes, a Gantt-style calendar UX prototype, and scope boundaries, to begin after M0 is accepted.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Written in Vietnamese; references domain logic source modules (agents/operational_prompts.py, agents/campaign_ideation.py, agents/campaign_scope_library.py) that the web layer must mirror.

fileNodes.name: output-reader-editor.md
fileNodes.summary: Spec for a dedicated full-width reading and editing experience for AI research outputs, covering a standalone '#doc/<run_id>' page, inline manual edits, AI-assisted ('Max') paragraph edits via surgical_edit.patch_document, and version history, with acceptance criteria for each feature.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Written in Vietnamese; ties feature F3 directly to the backend function agents/surgical_edit.patch_document and new API routes in webapp/api.py.

fileNodes.name: README.md
fileNodes.summary: Documentation for the Marketing OS web dashboard: a chat-first SPA centered on Max (virtual CMO) that guides users through 5 journey stages (discovery, diagnosis, strategy, production, operations), with API endpoints, backend setup, Telegram two-way control, real AI agent data wiring, Facebook Ads OAuth connection, and realtime SSE updates.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Written in Vietnamese; documents a hash-router SPA architecture plus a Starlette/SQLite backend (run_web.py) that mirrors bot/main.py's mounted API.

fileNodes.name: dashboard-standalone.html
fileNodes.summary: Single-file bundled version of the Marketing OS dashboard (HTML + inline CSS + inline JS) intended for offline use or static hosting (e.g., GitHub Pages) without requiring the backend server.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Inlines the same CSS custom-property design tokens found in web/styles.css, indicating it is a self-contained mirror of the modular index.html + styles.css + app.js setup.

fileNodes.name: index.html
fileNodes.summary: App shell for the Marketing OS dashboard SPA: defines the sidebar/main/rail layout skeleton, loads Chart.js, data.js, and app.js, and lets JavaScript render the hash-routed pages into #view.

fileNodes.name: styles.css
fileNodes.summary: Global stylesheet for the dashboard SPA defining the dark-themed design system (CSS custom properties for colors, radius, shadows) and layout/component styles used by index.html.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Claude Code skill guiding API and interface design, covering contract-first design, error semantics, validation at boundaries, REST patterns, and TypeScript interface conventions.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill describing how to test web apps in real browsers using the Chrome DevTools MCP server, including setup, installation, and available tools.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill defining the quality-gate CI/CD pipeline approach, including GitHub Actions configuration examples for automated build/test/deploy workflows.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill establishing a five-axis code review framework covering correctness, readability, simplicity, and other quality dimensions to apply before merging changes.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill outlining five principles for simplifying code without changing behavior, such as preserving behavior exactly and following project conventions.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill describing a layered context hierarchy (rules files, specs/architecture docs, etc.) for configuring agent context on a project.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill providing a systematic debugging methodology including a stop-the-line rule and a triage checklist for reproducing and fixing errors.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill covering principles for deprecating and migrating old systems or APIs, including the cost of code as a liability and Hyrum's Law implications for removal.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill explaining when and how to write Architecture Decision Records, including an ADR template, to capture context for future engineers.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill defining a doubt-driven development process that subjects decisions to adversarial review, including loading constraints and a claim-surfacing step.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill covering production-quality frontend UI engineering practices, including component architecture, file structure, and component design patterns.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill describing git workflow and versioning principles, recommending trunk-based development and frequent small commits.

fileNodes.name: idea-refine.sh
fileNodes.summary: Helper shell script for the idea-refine skill that ensures the docs/ideas directory exists and reports its readiness status as JSON.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill describing the incremental implementation cycle and slicing strategies, favoring vertical slices for building features step by step.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill defining an interview-style process for hypothesizing and confirming requirements with confidence scoring before proceeding with work.

fileNodes.name: LICENSE_agent-skills
fileNodes.summary: MIT license text for the vendored Agent Skills collection (by Addy Osmani) bundled under .claude/skills.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill guiding observability and instrumentation practices, including defining 'working' before instrumenting and choosing the right signal for each question.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill providing a performance optimization workflow with Core Web Vitals targets and a measure-first methodology for web performance work.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill describing the planning process for breaking down tasks, including entering plan mode and identifying the dependency graph of work.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill defining a threat-model-first security and hardening process, including a three-tier boundary system and a set of non-negotiable security rules.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill providing a pre-launch checklist covering code quality, security, and other readiness criteria before shipping a feature or release.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill describing a source-driven development process that detects the project's stack and versions and fetches official documentation to ground implementation decisions.

fileNodes.name: SOURCE.md
fileNodes.summary: Short attribution note (in Vietnamese) explaining that the .claude/skills process skills are vendored from Addy Osmani's open-source Agent Skills repo (MIT) to provide a process layer (spec to plan to build to test to review to ship) supplementing the project's business docs.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill defining a gated spec-driven development workflow with explicit specify and plan phases before implementation begins.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Skill describing the red-green-refactor TDD cycle, guiding writing a failing test first and then making it pass.

fileNodes.name: __init__.py
fileNodes.summary: Empty package marker for the services module; no executable content.

fileNodes.name: ads_notifier.py
fileNodes.summary: Builds and sends Facebook Ads performance digests and alerts: pulls/snapshots ad account metrics, computes deltas, formats daily/weekly/quick reports, detects threshold alerts, and safely sends Telegram messages.

fileNodes.name: ads_scheduler.py
fileNodes.summary: Background scheduler that periodically triggers daily/weekly ad digests, alert monitoring, token refresh, snapshot backfill, and cleanup jobs based on time-of-day checks.

fileNodes.name: fb_connections.py
fileNodes.summary: Data access layer for Facebook Ads account connections, OAuth state, ad snapshots, and alert cooldowns, backed by Supabase tables (user_fb_connections, oauth_states, ads_snapshots, ads_alert_cooldowns).

fileNodes.name: workflow_errors.py
fileNodes.summary: Logs structured workflow execution errors (user, task type, step, skill, message) to persistent storage for diagnostics.

fileNodes.name: plan.md
fileNodes.summary: Implementation plan (in Vietnamese/English) for an 'Output Reader & Editor' feature, outlining dependency graph, reused infrastructure, tasks T1-T4 (read page, manual edit, version history, AI-assisted edit), and two review checkpoints.

fileNodes.name: SKILL.md
fileNodes.summary: Operating guide for Claude Code's agent-skills system, covering skill discovery, core operating behaviors (surfacing assumptions, managing confusion, pushing back, enforcing simplicity, scope discipline, verification), and the skill lifecycle sequence.

fileNodes.name: .nojekyll
fileNodes.summary: Empty marker file that disables GitHub Pages' Jekyll processing for the static web assets in this repo.

fileNodes.name: .python-version
fileNodes.summary: Pins the Python interpreter version used by pyenv/tooling for local development and CI consistency.

fileNodes.name: campaign_intake_prompts.py
fileNodes.summary: Builds LLM user-prompt strings for the campaign intake and funnel-mapper agent stages, assembling brand-voice, strategy, and profile context into structured prompt text.

fileNodes.name: campaign_scope_library.py
fileNodes.summary: Static library of per-industry campaign scope definitions with lookup and prompt-formatting helpers used to ground campaign-planning agents in industry-specific scope.

fileNodes.name: discovery_prompts.py
fileNodes.summary: Builds LLM user-prompt strings for the market-research and brief-generation stages of the discovery agent pipeline.

fileNodes.name: social_industry_profiles.py
fileNodes.summary: Static per-industry social-media content library (including TikTok content lines) with lookup helpers used by content-generation agents.

fileNodes.name: surgical_edit.py
fileNodes.summary: Implements targeted document editing: splits a generated document into numbered sections, detects which section(s) a user comment targets via an LLM call, rewrites just those sections, and reassembles the patched document.

fileNodes.name: task_workflows.py
fileNodes.summary: Defines the WorkflowStep data class describing a single step (skill, persona, required context keys) within a multi-step agent task workflow.

fileNodes.name: excel_reader.py
fileNodes.summary: Reads uploaded .xlsx files and converts worksheet contents into markdown tables, with filename-based skill detection used to route uploaded spreadsheets to the correct task workflow.

fileNodes.name: html_report.py
fileNodes.summary: Generates the full HTML marketing-strategy and ads-dashboard reports delivered to users, including markdown-to-HTML conversion, tabbed multi-stage layouts, bar charts/tables, and an LLM-generated archetype banner.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of string templating and regex-based markdown post-processing rather than a templating engine.

fileNodes.name: content_generation_template.xlsx
fileNodes.summary: Excel workbook template used as a structured input/output format for the content-generation workflow (e.g. content calendar or campaign plan rows).

fileNodes.name: pricing_segment_library.py
fileNodes.summary: Static library of per-industry pricing segment definitions with lookup and prompt-formatting helpers used by pricing-strategy agents.

fileNodes.name: todo.md
fileNodes.summary: Task checklist (Vietnamese) for the 'Output Reader & Editor' feature, breaking down T1-T4 (read page, manual edit, version history, AI-assisted edit) into actionable items.

fileNodes.name: __init__.py
fileNodes.summary: Empty package marker for the tests module; no executable content.

fileNodes.name: test_content_pipeline_e2e.py
fileNodes.summary: End-to-end test suite (16 scenarios) verifying content generation pipeline behaviors: calendar/tone/business-view injection into skill prompts, weekly scope handling, and validation rules, using fake profile/session/skill/business-view doubles and a custom async test runner.

fileNodes.name: __init__.py
fileNodes.summary: Empty package marker for the tools module; no executable content.

fileNodes.name: image_prompt_library.py
fileNodes.summary: Library of reusable image-generation prompt templates categorized by product type, with helpers to categorize input text, pick a matching template, and build a final formatted image prompt.

fileNodes.name: krillin_client.py
fileNodes.summary: Client for extracting audio/video transcripts via a local Krillin CLI tool or fallback OpenAI Whisper API, handling remote file download, availability checks, and prompt-ready transcript formatting.

fileNodes.name: business.py
fileNodes.summary: Real business-data layer for the web dashboard: reads live Supabase tables shared with the Telegram bot (profile, campaigns, competitors, brand voice, skill runs) and triggers real analysis/skill pipelines from agents/.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Falls back gracefully to a disabled state ({'bizEnabled': False}) when Supabase credentials are absent, keeping the frontend decoupled from backend availability.

fileNodes.name: chat.py
fileNodes.summary: Implements Max, the web dashboard's AI marketing-advisor chatbot, including conversation history persistence, stage-aware suggestions, and turn handling that can trigger real analysis skills.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Reuses bot primitives (agents.discovery.run_discovery_turn, tools.llm_router.call, webapp.business.run_agent) to keep web and Telegram bot behavior consistent.

fileNodes.name: notify.py
fileNodes.summary: Best-effort bridge that sends Telegram notifications for actions taken on the web dashboard, using the Telegram Bot API directly via httpx.

fileNodes.name: store_sqlite.py
fileNodes.summary: Default SQLite-backed storage layer for the web dashboard's mock/demo state (tracked competitors, jobs, campaigns, accounts, alerts), used when Supabase credentials are not configured.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Exposes an async-style interface mirroring the Supabase REST backend so the rest of the webapp can treat both backends interchangeably.

fileNodes.name: web_tracked
fileNodes.summary: Tracks competitor accounts being monitored from the web dashboard (id, name, ads count, status, last-seen).

fileNodes.name: web_jobs
fileNodes.summary: Stores scheduled background jobs (name, schedule text, on/off status) shown in the dashboard's automation view.

fileNodes.name: web_optimizations
fileNodes.summary: Stores suggested ad-optimization actions (scale, pause, duplicate, activate) with rationale, surfaced in the optimizer view.

fileNodes.name: web_alerts
fileNodes.summary: Stores dashboard alerts with severity, icon, title, and metadata for the alerts rail.

fileNodes.name: web_settings
fileNodes.summary: Key-value settings table storing dashboard configuration values such as token quota.

fileNodes.name: web_campaigns
fileNodes.summary: Stores marketing campaigns with name, status, budget, and objective for the campaign management views.

fileNodes.name: web_calendar_posts
fileNodes.summary: Stores content calendar posts with day, content pillar, and title for the content calendar view.

fileNodes.name: web_content_items
fileNodes.summary: Stores generated content items (hook, format, status) produced by the content generator feature.

fileNodes.name: web_reports
fileNodes.summary: Stores generated reports (name, type, date) listed in the dashboard's reports view.

fileNodes.name: web_accounts
fileNodes.summary: Stores connected ad accounts (name, account id, status, spend) for the accounts/integrations view.

fileNodes.name: web_users
fileNodes.summary: Stores web dashboard users with plan, token quota, and usage for per-user billing/quota tracking.

fileNodes.name: web_chat
fileNodes.summary: Stores persisted Max chat messages (user id, role, content, timestamp) backing the dashboard's AI advisor chat history.

fileNodes.name: supabase_schema.sql
fileNodes.summary: Defines the Supabase schema for the web dashboard's mock-first web_* tables (tracked competitors, jobs, optimizations, alerts, campaigns, calendar posts, content items, reports, accounts, users, chat) plus a chat index.

fileNodes.name: __init__.py
fileNodes.summary: Package marker for the workers module, which hosts background cron-style scheduled tasks.

fileNodes.name: token_billing.py
fileNodes.summary: Computes LLM usage cost in USD and VND across providers and formats cost summary reports from the token log.

fileNodes.name: token_tracker.py
fileNodes.summary: Tracks per-job and per-skill LLM token usage and quota, persisting usage logs and producing usage summaries/footers shown to users.

fileNodes.name: .nojekyll
fileNodes.summary: Empty marker file that disables GitHub Pages' Jekyll processing for the static web dashboard.

fileNodes.name: app.js
fileNodes.summary: Client-side single-page application logic for the Marketing OS web dashboard: hash-based routing, chart rendering, business data fetching, AI chat with Max, and rendering of strategy/calendar/campaign views.
fileNodes.languageNotes: Large vanilla-JS SPA using string-templated HTML rendering, a tiny hash router, and a thin fetch wrapper (API.get/post/del) rather than a framework.

fileNodes.name: data.js
fileNodes.summary: Defines the mock data and navigation/journey configuration (window.MOCK) used to populate the web dashboard before real backend data is wired in.

fileNodes.name: build_standalone.py
fileNodes.summary: Build script that inlines the web dashboard's CSS and JS into a single standalone HTML file for static hosting (e.g., GitHub Pages).
